name: La Tiñosa Mountains and Arranca Valley Trail
name_original: Corrales de La Tiñosa — circular desde Vertavillo
description: Easy 10.0-mile mountain bike route through a mosaic landscape, passing the historic Corrales de La Tiñosa near Vertavillo.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Vertavillo are found within the Cerrato region of Palencia, Castilla y León, offering diverse landscapes for outdoor activities. The area is characterized by an elevated plateau with extensive panoramic views and rolling terrain. This mosaic landscape provides varied backdrops for mountain biking, featuring sections of paths, singletrack, and street. Best mountain bike trails around Vertavillo The most popular mountain bike trail is La Tiñosa Mountains and Arranca Valley Trail, an easy 10.0 miles (16.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 28 minutes to complete. This route features a mosaic landscape and passes by the historic Corrales de La Tiñosa. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is La Virgen del Llano a Cevico de la Torre a Vertavillo — circular desde Cubillas de Cerrato, a moderate 26.1 miles (42.1 km) path. This route offers varied terrain across the rolling landscape of the region. Local mountain bikers also love the Senda del Chorro Trail, a 13.5 miles (21.7 km) trail leading through the diverse terrain of Vertavillo, often completed in about 1 hour 54 minutes. Mountain biking around Vertavillo is defined by its elevated plateau, rolling terrain, and mosaic landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging moderate routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Vertavillo?
A: Vertavillo offers a selection of 4 mountain bike trails, catering to various skill levels. These routes traverse the diverse landscapes of the Cerrato region, providing panoramic views and varied terrain.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Vertavillo?
A: Yes, beginners can enjoy the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1159462618/la-tinosa-mountains-and-arranca-valley-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Tiñosa Mountains and Arranca Valley Trail&lt;/a&gt;. This easy 10.0-mile (16.1 km) route features a mosaic landscape and passes by the historic Corrales de La Tiñosa, making it a great option for those new to mountain biking or looking for a relaxed ride.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in the Vertavillo area?
A: Absolutely. A notable circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1324118909/la-virgen-del-llano-a-cevico-de-la-torre-a-vertavillo-circular-desde-cubillas-de-cerrato" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Virgen del Llano a Cevico de la Torre a Vertavillo — circular desde Cubillas de Cerrato&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate 26.1-mile (42.1 km) path offers varied terrain across the region's rolling landscape. Another circular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1324172694/hermedes-de-cerrato-a-castrillo-de-don-juan-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hérmedes de Cerrato a Castrillo de Don Juan — circular&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 17.9-mile (28.8 km) trail.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while mountain biking in Vertavillo?
A: The Vertavillo region is characterized by its 'mosaic landscape' and 'rolling terrain.' You'll encounter an elevated plateau offering extensive panoramic views, with routes featuring sections of paths, singletrack, and street, providing a varied and scenic riding experience.

Q: Are there any historical sites or attractions along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1159462618/la-tinosa-mountains-and-arranca-valley-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Tiñosa Mountains and Arranca Valley Trail&lt;/a&gt; passes by the historic Corrales de La Tiñosa. Additionally, Vertavillo itself boasts rich historical architecture, including the Church of San Miguel Arcángel and the Ermita del Santo Cristo del Consuelo.

Q: What are some interesting places to explore near Vertavillo after a ride?
A: Beyond the trails, you can explore several attractions. Consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4102610" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villafuerte de Esgueva Castle&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5797677" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Miguel Arcángel and Rollo de Vertavillo&lt;/a&gt;, or the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4688366" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cevico de la Torre Ceramic Jug Sculpture&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is Vertavillo a good destination for birdwatching while cycling?
A: Yes, the region is known for its ornithological interest. The 'Monte de Vertavillo Route' (which can be cycled) is particularly noted for birdwatching, where you might spot species like the blue elanio, royal eagle, griffon vulture, and various larks and warblers.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike trail in Vertavillo?
A: The longest mountain bike trail in the area is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1324118909/la-virgen-del-llano-a-cevico-de-la-torre-a-vertavillo-circular-desde-cubillas-de-cerrato" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Virgen del Llano a Cevico de la Torre a Vertavillo — circular desde Cubillas de Cerrato&lt;/a&gt;, covering a distance of 26.1 miles (42.1 km).

Q: Are there any moderate difficulty mountain bike trails for more experienced riders?
A: Yes, there are several moderate trails for those seeking a bit more challenge. These include the 26.1-mile (42.1 km) &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1324118909/la-virgen-del-llano-a-cevico-de-la-torre-a-vertavillo-circular-desde-cubillas-de-cerrato" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Virgen del Llano a Cevico de la Torre a Vertavillo — circular desde Cubillas de Cerrato&lt;/a&gt;, the 13.5-mile (21.7 km) &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1323629735/senda-del-chorro-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Chorro Trail&lt;/a&gt;, and the 18.5-mile (29.7 km) &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1324800471/monastery-of-san-pelayo-cevico-navero-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of San Pelayo - Cevico Navero Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What makes mountain biking in Vertavillo unique?
A: Mountain biking in Vertavillo is unique due to its location on an elevated plateau within the Cerrato region, offering extensive panoramic views. The 'mosaic landscape' provides a diverse backdrop, combining natural beauty with historical elements like the Corrales de La Tiñosa and the rich cultural heritage of Vertavillo itself.
tips: Vertavillo is a charming municipality in the province of Palencia, Castilla y León. This village, located in the Cerrato region, offers a rich history and an impressive natural environment.
Traditional constructions linked to the ramaderia. They are frequent throughout the Cerrato region, although they stopped being used in the mid-20th century.

faqs.question: Is the Corrales de La Tiñosa — circular desde Vertavillo trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this mountain biking trail is considered easy and is suitable for all skill levels and any fitness level. It's a great option for those new to mountain biking.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route traverses a diverse 'mosaic landscape' featuring cereal crops, patches of Mediterranean forest, and pastures-thickets. You'll encounter typical vegetation like holm oaks, kermes oaks, savin junipers, and junipers. There's also an unfarmed valley floor with splendid pastureland and an oaky holm oak forest.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the trail?
faqs.answer: A key point of interest is the Corrales de La Tiñosa themselves, which are traditional stone constructions reflecting the area's historical livestock farming. You'll also pass by the Church of San Miguel Arcángel and Rollo de Vertavillo near the start/end of the route.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is situated within the Montes del Cerrato Community Interest Site, known for its beauty and biodiversity. While specific seasonal advice isn't provided, spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures for mountain biking and excellent opportunities for birdwatching, which is a notable activity in this area.

faqs.question: Is the Corrales de La Tiñosa — circular desde Vertavillo trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally suitable for dogs, but always ensure your dog is kept under control, preferably on a leash, to protect local wildlife and respect other trail users. The area is known for birdwatching, so particular care should be taken.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access the trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Corrales de La Tiñosa — circular desde Vertavillo trail. It's freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Where can I park when visiting the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Vertavillo. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, you can typically find parking within the village itself, close to the trailhead. Consider parking near the Church of San Miguel Arcángel.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The area is particularly notable for birdwatching, being part of the Montes del Cerrato Community Interest Site. You might spot species like the Montagu's harrier, golden eagle, griffon vulture, and various other raptors and passerines.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Cañada Real Burgalesa.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or pubs near Vertavillo?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is rural, Vertavillo is a village where you may find basic amenities. It's advisable to check local listings for cafes or pubs in Vertavillo before your visit, as options might be limited.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its 'easy' difficulty rating and suitability for all skill levels, this circular mountain biking route is generally considered family-friendly. The diverse scenery and points of interest like the Corrales de La Tiñosa can make it an enjoyable outing for families.

waypoints.name: Church of San Miguel Arcángel and Rollo de Vertavillo
